methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private-market peers in the same sector and stage—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a $285M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks against the five same-sector, same-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $310M and a range from $120M to $500M, yielding a $310M implied valuation. The two methods converge within a reasonable band.

Synthesizing across methods produces a triangulated range of $242M to $357M, with a median of $298M. This range reflects material dispersion in the peer set—particularly the $380M spread between Cemvita and Pano AI—indicating that stage, technology maturity, and capital efficiency remain meaningful valuation drivers within the climate tech subsector. Key uncertainties include the target's specific technology differentiation relative to peers, unit economics, and pathway to positive cash flow, all of which would warrant downward or upward adjustment from the $242M–$357M range.
